perturbation.original_text: Products. The term "Products" means and includes the Products to be distributed by the Master Distributor under the Master Distributorship Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Products. The term "Products" means and includes any items the Master Distributor chooses to distribute, irrespective of the Master Distributorship Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Products' in Section 1(n) limits it to those distributed under the Master Distributorship Agreement. The modified version broadens the definition to 'any items the Master Distributor chooses to distribute, irrespective of the Master Distributorship Agreement.' This creates a contradiction because it implies that the Master Distributor can distribute items outside the scope of the agreed-upon agreement, creating an in-text contradiction with the initial definiton where 'Products' means and includes the Products to be distributed by the Master Distributor under the Master Distributorship Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Products. The term "Products" means and includes any items the Master Distributor chooses to distribute, irrespective of the Master Distributorship Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Products' as those distributed under the Master Distributorship Agreement. The changed text broadens the definition to include any items the Master Distributor chooses to distribute, irrespective of the agreement. The contradicted text uses the broad definition from the changed text, which directly conflicts with the original, narrow definition, as the Changed Text directly contradicts the Original Text's definition. This makes it a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Master Distributor agrees and acknowledges that all Proprietary Information, Intellectual Property, Trade Secrets and Confidential Information, and any and all embodiments thereof (herein, the "Property"), is and shall remain at all times the exclusive property of and owned by the Company, and that Master Distributor's performance of its duties and obligations and its use or awareness of the Property during the term of this Agreement or the Master Distributorship Agreement shall create no rights, at law or in equity, under the laws and decisions of any nation or state within the Territory, in Master Distributor in or to the Property, or any aspect or embodiment thereof. Master Distributor further agrees not to copy, reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile or otherwise reproduce any Product or its equivalent, in whole or in part.
perturbation.changed_text: Master Distributor agrees and acknowledges that all Proprietary Information, Intellectual Property, Trade Secrets and Confidential Information, and any and all embodiments thereof (herein, the "Property"), is and shall remain at all times the exclusive property of and owned by the Company, except for any improvements or modifications made by Master Distributor, which shall be jointly owned. Master Distributor's performance of its duties and obligations and its use or awareness of the Property during the term of this Agreement or the Master Distributorship Agreement shall create no rights, at law or in equity, under the laws and decisions of any nation or state within the Territory, in Master Distributor in or to the Property, or any aspect or embodiment thereof. Master Distributor further agrees not to copy, reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile or otherwise reproduce any Product or its equivalent, in whole or in part.
perturbation.explanation: Section 3 originally states that all Proprietary Information, Intellectual Property, Trade Secrets, and Confidential Information are exclusively owned by the Company. The modification introduces an exception, stating that 'any improvements or modifications made by Master Distributor shall be jointly owned.' This creates a direct contradiction regarding the ownership of any improvements or modifications. The original agreement says 'all times the exclusive property of and owned by the Company' and the perturbation says that any improvements shall be jointly owned. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Master Distributor agrees and acknowledges that all Proprietary Information, Intellectual Property, Trade Secrets and Confidential Information, and any and all embodiments thereof (herein, the "Property"), is and shall remain at all times the exclusive property of and owned by the Company, except for any improvements or modifications made by Master Distributor, which shall be jointly owned. Master Distributor's performance of its duties and obligations and its use or awareness of the Property during the term of this Agreement or the Master Distributorship Agreement shall create no rights, at law or in equity, under the laws and decisions of any nation or state within the Territory, in Master Distributor in or to the Property, or any aspect or embodiment thereof. Master Distributor further agrees not to copy, reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile or otherwise reproduce any Product or its equivalent, in whole or in part.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text to state that 'any improvements or modifications made by Master Distributor shall be jointly owned,' while the contradicted text (which is identical to the changed text in this case, but that doesn't change the determination) asserts that all Proprietary Information is exclusively owned by the Company. This directly contradicts the earlier statement, creating an in-text contradiction regarding ownership of improvements or modifications.

perturbation.original_text: Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information or Proprietary Information. Master Distributor shall not, during the term of this Agreement and the Master Distributorship Agreement, and for a period of three (3) years following termination, for any reason, of the Master Distributorship Agreement, disclose, use, reveal, report, publish, disclose, transfer, or make available, directly or indirectly, to any person, business concern, or other entity, any Confidential Information or Proprietary Information except in the proper performance of its duties hereunder; provided, however, Master Distributor is not prohibited hereby from disclosing or using any Confidential Information or Proprietary Information which subsequently becomes part of the public domain through no breach of this Agreement and the Master Distributorship Agreement and through no fault of Master Distributor.
perturbation.changed_text: Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information or Proprietary Information. Master Distributor shall not, during the term of this Agreement and the Master Distributorship Agreement, disclose, use, reveal, report, publish, disclose, transfer, or make available, directly or indirectly, to any person, business concern, or other entity, any Confidential Information or Proprietary Information except in the proper performance of its duties hereunder; provided, however, Master Distributor is explicitly permitted to disclose Confidential Information or Proprietary Information to its subsidiaries and affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: Section 5 limits the non-disclosure of Confidential Information and Proprietary Information for three years after termination. The modified version permits Master Distributor to disclose this information to its subsidiaries and affiliates. This introduces a contradiction, because the original text states that the Master Distributor shall not disclose said information for a period of three years, whereas the perturbed text says that is explicitly permitted to disclose Confidential Information or Proprietary Information to its subsidiaries and affiliates. This creates an in-text contradiction and uncertainty about who can and cannot receive the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Master Distributor shall not, during the term of this Agreement and the Master Distributorship Agreement, disclose, use, reveal, report, publish, disclose, transfer, or make available, directly or indirectly, to any person, business concern, or other entity, any Confidential Information or Proprietary Information except in the proper performance of its duties hereunder; provided, however, Master Distributor is explicitly permitted to disclose Confidential Information or Proprietary Information to its subsidiaries and affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 5 strictly prohibits the Master Distributor from disclosing Confidential Information or Proprietary Information for three years after termination. The changed text modifies this section to explicitly permit the Master Distributor to disclose this information to its subsidiaries and affiliates. This creates a direct contradiction with the original intent of Section 5, which was to prevent any disclosure for a specified period. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but it's the change that creates the contradiction with the original intent of non-disclosure, as described in the Perturbation Explanation. Thus, it's a clear in-text contradiction.
